moves.note: Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero
Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero
Add quality tech, strong scanner setup

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and meet the mandate.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL holdings to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure.
Reduce QQQ and SMH due to downtrends and to meet mandate limits.
Increase QQQI to 25% to favor income and broad market exposure.
Buy ZS at 5% as a new quality tech holding from the strong scanner setups.
This plan reduces overall leverage and concentration, favoring capital preservation.
conservative.read: This conservative plan aims for a target of ~1.5% per month, prioritizing capital preservation. Risk is significantly reduced by cutting leveraged ETFs and lowering concentration, but potential upside is also limited. Expect lower volatility and drawdown compared to other modes.

moves.note: Add quality tech, strong scanner setup

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate.
Sell SOXL and SPAL to reduce leveraged ETF exposure to zero.
Reduce QQQ and SMH holdings due to downtrends and to manage overall risk.
Buy NVDU at 10% and ZS at 5% from the strong scanner setups.
This plan balances broad index exposure with quality tech and reduced leverage.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets ~2.5% per month, focusing on risk-first growth. Leverage is reduced to zero, and concentration is managed, aiming for a blend of broad market and quality tech. Expect moderate volatility and drawdown.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate.
Sell SOXL and SPAL due to being below stops and reallocate capital.
Reduce QQQ and SMH exposure due to downtrends.
Buy NVDU at 15% and AMZU at 10% from the highest-scoring scanner setups.
This plan focuses on pressing what is working and concentrating in strong tech setups.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets ~4% per month, aiming for stretch growth. It involves concentrating in high-scoring setups and using leverage (implied by higher concentration in growth names) for increased upside. This approach carries significantly higher drawdown risk and potential for rapid losses, requiring strict stop discipline.

moves.note: Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero
Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and manage risk.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL holdings to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure.
Sell QQQ and SMH to reduce growth exposure and focus on income.
Trim CRWD to 5% to reduce high-beta single name exposure.
Increase QQQI to 40% to prioritize income and monthly distributions.
This plan rotates into income-focused ETFs for steady distributions.
income.read: This income plan targets ~1% per month, aiming to park cash for yield. It prioritizes low drawdown and reliable income over growth, rotating into income/covered-call ETFs. Distributions are variable and NAV can still fall in a selloff; this is not a savings account but aims for cash-like income.
